assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md line 46: 'Endpoint handler pattern (from `get.rs` siblings)' references analyzing get.rs. conventions.md line 18: 'All handlers return `Result<T, AppError>` where `AppError` is defined in `common/src/error.rs`' references common/src/error.rs. plan.md line 89: 'Entity `AdvisorySummary.severity`: referenced in Implementation Notes at `advisory/model/summary.rs`' references summary.rs. file-5-description.md line 62: 'The implementation must inspect the existing `get.rs` handler to determine the exact extraction pattern and replicate it.' explicitly calls for inspecting get.rs. At least 3 of the 4 specified files are referenced for inspection/analysis.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 16-33 list exactly 3 files to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs) and 3 files to create (advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, tests/api/advisory_summary.rs). plan.md line 95 explicitly states: 'All modified and created files are within the scope defined by the task’s "Files to Modify" and "Files to Create" sections. No out-of-scope changes are needed.' No other files are listed for modification or creation.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 25: 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs' (model, confirmed by file-4-description.md Action: CREATE). plan.md line 27: 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs' (endpoint handler, confirmed by file-5-description.md Action: CREATE). plan.md line 29: 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' (test file, confirmed by file-6-description.md Action: CREATE). All three files are present with CREATE action.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 17: 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' (confirmed by file-1-description.md Action: MODIFY). plan.md line 19: 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs' (confirmed by file-2-description.md Action: MODIFY). plan.md line 21: 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs' (confirmed by file-3-description.md Action: MODIFY). All three files are present with MODIFY action.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 52-60 show the commit message: 'feat(api): add advisory severity aggregation endpoint' followed by a body and footer 'Implements TC-9201'. The format 'feat(api): description' is valid Conventional Commits (type=feat, scope=api). TC-9201 is referenced in the footer line.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 62: 'With `--trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"`.' This is stated immediately after the commit message block, indicating the trailer flag should be used when committing.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md has a '## Target Branch' section (line 8-9) identifying 'main' as the target branch, which satisfies the second part. However, there is no mention anywhere in any output file of creating a branch named TC-9201, no branch naming convention is discussed, and no branch creation step is included in the plan. The plan omits the branch creation requirement entirely.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md line 6: 'Every domain module follows a strict `model/ + service/ + endpoints/` tripartite structure.' identifies the module structure pattern. conventions.md lines 18-19: 'All handlers return `Result<T, AppError>` where `AppError` is defined in `common/src/error.rs`. Errors are wrapped with `.context()` to add descriptive messages at each call site.' identifies the error handling pattern with both Result<T, AppError> and .context(). Both patterns are explicitly identified.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 8-9 contain '## Target Branch' followed by 'main'. This clearly extracts and identifies the target branch value from the task description.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: No mention of 'digest', 'description digest', 'Step 1.5', or 'description-digest-protocol' appears anywhere in any of the output files (plan.md, conventions.md, or any file-N-description.md). The plan completely omits this requirement.
